migrations.note: dev-bumped during plugin authorship (informal, not via /starter-kit:migrate)
V0.5 intent capture: backfilled brief/00-INTENT.md and docs/00-VISION.md retroactively from the original prompt
V0.6 add /starter-kit:verify-bootstrap skill
V0.7 add optional specialized docs (DATA_MODEL, SECURITY, DESIGN_SYSTEM, ROADMAP, I18N); de-number entry docs: brief/00-INTENT.md -> brief/INTENT.md, docs/00-VISION.md -> docs/VISION.md (ADR 0007)
V0.8 English-only: dropped bilingual FR/EN templates + {{LANG}} logic, translated skills/docs to English (ADR 0012)
V0.9 moved media/ -> docs/media/ to avoid collision with project media/public folders (ADR 0013)
V0.10 adopt always offers optional/specialized docs (Call 3b); living-docs maintenance rule in generated CLAUDE.md
V0.10.1 meta/docs only: documented the batch-small-changes release cadence (no user-facing change)
V0.11 renamed brief/ -> intake/ (ADR 0014): dogfood git mv brief intake + reference sweep

V0.12 best-effort update check in bootstrap/adopt/migrate (ADR 0015); marketplace renamed starter-kit-local -> claude-code-starter-kit (ADR 0016); public contact email; ADR 0017 accepted (rename to groundrules at V1.0.0)
V1.0 plugin renamed starter-kit -> groundrules (ADR 0017): state file .starter-kit.json -> .groundrules.json, key starterKitVersion -> groundrulesVersion, signatures and command prefix swept
V1.1 adopt consolidate mode (ADR 0018); PROCESS/RELEASE optional docs + LEARNINGS rule format + Session Start (ADR 0019, harvested from crm-heyjoe); repo-is-the-only-memory convention (ADR 0020)

V1.2 context-economy guide + ADR 0021; agent-evals optional doc + checkpoint-capture ritual + /groundrules:checkpoint skill + ADR 0022; graphify interop pointer; learn skill aligned to rule format; README Why+MIT+restructure
V1.3 new skill /groundrules:slim (CLAUDE.md 200-line optimizer, ADR 0024); team-portability project-scope guidance in bootstrap/adopt + README (ADR 0023)
V1.3.1 doc patch: hardened 'two-step plugin update' guidance (README + Phase 0 notices); adopt header fix; LEARNINGS+AGENT-EVALS capture of the marketplace-update-≠-plugin-update trap
V1.3.2 README polish: 'What it buys you' benefits summary under Why

V1.3.3 verify-bootstrap robustness: exempt .gitignore from signature check (fresh-project E2E found false positive) + explicit backtick rule for placeholders; ADR 0025 closes PreToolUse hook + /watch-bootstrap as won't-do

V1.4 per-feature PRD + Posture (ADR 0026); reflection/realization method + interactive/loop regimes + loop-readiness roadmap (ADR 0027); git workflow conventions: neutral branching, boundary commits, AI attribution-unless-forbidden (ADR 0028); new /groundrules:idea skill; checkpoint surface-drift bucket; superpowers interop sharpened (verified facts in LEARNINGS); README research/established-practices/superpowers sections; docs/ROADMAP.md created

V1.5 new /groundrules:vision skill (guided VISION interview, create-if-absent/refine-in-place); content-aware CLAUDE.md tailoring retires CLAUDE.lean.md.tpl (ADR 0029 supersedes 0009 — omit only global-covered sections, bias-to-keep, omission list + veto; adopt additions gap-driven); adopt opt-in docs/ADOPTION-LOG.md (field->plugin feedback channel); superpowers research captured in LEARNINGS + ROADMAP M1 verifier/maker contract + M3 dashboard. Features validated by fresh-subagent E2Es.

V1.6 M1 loop-readiness: opt-in maker/verifier loop scaffolding in bootstrap/adopt (loop/ namespace, ADR 0030) + conditional ## Invariants; new /groundrules:realize skill (approved plan -> partitioned [loop]/[supervised] backlog) with a TDD-before-loop gate (pre-written red acceptance test, writer != maker); blocked.md backward-crossing triage convention; CLAUDE.md 'Claude Code workflow' routes work by regime (ADR 0027). Validated by adversarial fresh-subagent E2Es; loop prototype in docs/prototypes/loop/.

V1.6.1 docs/tests only (no plugin behaviour change): three-tier loop guide test/loop/ (QUICKSTART -> TUTORIAL[Conway Game of Life, Go oracle] -> WALKTHROUGH) + deterministic validate-runner.sh (stubs claude to assert run-loop.sh MAX cap/DONE stop) + fixtures; ADR 0031 /goal interop (swappable loop executor: /goal=light, groundrules loop=high-fidelity); README 'Updating the plugin' moved under Installation. ADR index 0030+0031 backfilled.

V1.7 premortem & anti-sycophancy (ADR 0032): new skill /groundrules:premortem (assume-it-failed adversarial pass, the reflection-side twin of the loop verifier) + premortem hint in PRD.md.tpl Risks + premortem pointer in CLAUDE.md Posture; README sycophancy row refreshed with verified 2026 evidence (Science ~50%, AISI reframing>instruction) alongside Sharma 2023; honest sourcing (no +30%-on-LLM). Fix: commit the acceptance test before the loop (field-test). README intro 'shows its work' hook.

answers.description: Plugin Claude Code qui bootstrappe de nouveaux projets via un slash command interactif.

intent.goal: Construire un plugin Claude Code partageable qui standardise l'amorçage de nouveaux projets via un slash command interactif. Génère structure de doc (CLAUDE.md, docs/, ADR, learnings, intake, vision), git init, remote optionnel, application dynamique des bonnes pratiques fetchées en ligne.
intent.users: Guillaume Ferrari (solo) + communauté Claude Code installant via la marketplace publique sur GitHub (lozit/claude-code-groundrules).
constraints: Plugin Claude Code natif (.claude-plugin/, skills/), pas de runtime externe
Pas de moteur de templates — substitution texte simple {{KEY}} (cf. ADR 0002)
Bilingue FR/EN sur tous les templates utilisateur
Fonctionne majoritairement offline (WebFetch seulement dans apply-best-practices)
nonGoals: Ne remplace pas l'écriture du CLAUDE.md (c'est un starter, pas un générateur)
Pas de gestion CI/CD (pas de templates GitHub Actions/GitLab CI)
Pas de support pour tooling non-Claude-Code (Cursor, Continue, etc.)
Pas de génération de code applicatif (pas de squelette React/Python/etc.)
Pas d'IA propriétaire pour la synthèse — Claude lui-même fait le boulot
acceptanceCriteria: Bootstrap d'un nouveau projet en < 5 minutes
7 skills opérationnels : bootstrap, adopt, add-adr, learn, migrate, apply-best-practices, verify-bootstrap
Structure générée complète (README, CLAUDE.md, docs/, intake/, docs/media/, conditionnels)
Intent capturable au bootstrap (brief paste / fichier / interview / skip)
apply-best-practices fetche shanraisshan, filtre par vision, propose multi-select
Plugin publié sur GitHub marketplace publique
Dogfood : le repo groundrules utilise sa propre structure et vision

appliedPractices.source: shanraisshan/claude-code-best-practice + howborisusesclaudecode.com (manual integration during V0.2 authorship)
appliedPractices.note: Manually integrated in CLAUDE.md.{fr,en}.tpl during V0.2 dev — not via /starter-kit:apply-best-practices since that skill didn't exist yet. Future runs of apply-best-practices on this repo would diff against these and propose new items.

items.name: Mettre à jour ce fichier (philosophie Boris)
.claude/settings.json, .claude/rules/*.md, <important if=...> tags
items.status: applied (mentioned, not yet exercised in dogfood)

appliedPractices.note: First real run of apply-best-practices on the dogfood repo. 12 recommendations returned; 4 were already-existing skills (filtered out as redundant), 2 redundant with V0.2 manual integration, 6 truly new and user-selected (4 applied, 2 deferred to V0.6).

items.name: Permissions preset (git, gh, glab, common bash, WebFetch domains)
